title: lewisia pygmaea subsp. longipetala
intro: Lewisia pygmaea subsp. longipetala is a plant species, commonly known as the Long-petaled Bitterroot, which belongs to the Portulacaceae family. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant's roots and leaves possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The Long-petaled Bitterroot offers various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
